title: dianthus plumarius subsp. plumarius
intro: Dianthus plumarius subsp. plumarius, commonly known as the fragrant pink or wild pink, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Dianthus plumarius subsp. plumarius include its ability to alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
